LDTEXT:
-on site to fix sand filter 1 module 2 which was not backwashing sand properly
-this is in response to the high total phosphorus in the comp sample for the lab earlier today, which was 0.088, which is above the objective value of 0.070. 
-backwashing lance which cleans sand was clogged, pulled lance, cleared any bigger particles and reinstated lance, restored air flow and backwashing of sand looked normal, this was my response to being above the internal objective, sample was still below compliance objective of 0.1 mg/L TP
-will return tomorrow for the Sunday lab, will check composite sample and see if Total phosphorus is trending downward
-sand filter 1, module 1 seems to be backwashing but slightly underperforming as well, perhaps this week it can be investigated as well
-currently both module 1 and 2 are backwashing
-notified ROC of work, had CMU5 W11407901 for sand filter 1 maintenance which I returned to site for
-W11407732 for Saturday Lab earlier in the day
DESCRIPTION: Mount Albert Water Resource Recovery Facility

ROC called regarding CT not achieved alarm @04:30.  Wells had interlocked, requested that ROC leave wells / alarms until 07:30. 
Incident overview: @04:25 - ROC manual started wells 1 and 2, @04:28 the wells 1 and 2 interlocked on effluent free chlorine low and CT not achieved.  Effluent free was 0.35 at the time of the incident, the analyzer was reading 0.35 mg/L before the wells started.  During the period that the wells were left off overnight, distribution water had been pulled back to the effluent analyzer - When the wells started, the CT not achieved alarm was generated as the chlorine residual was not high enough to meet the CT requirements.  The same incident had occured on October 13th, 2025, where we had filled for adverse water quality, so filed another adverse for todays incident

Reviewed trends @07:40. Manual started well #2 @07:59.  By 08:12, the effluent chlorine residual is back to 1.63 mg/L, the effluent required is 0.31 mg/L, no further issues with CT.  @08:13 - manual started well #1

Onsite today to assist contractor the modifications of DMP1 and DMP2.  Contractor Sub damaged incoming damper motherboard during service, causing the valve to be inoperable.
Needed to add bypass code for Local/Remote and Fault status.  System is operating as per normal now, with the damper 1 in LOCAL, OPEN to 100%.  If a Train shuts down, the valve will remain open as the damper control has been left in LOCAL due to damage.  If the power is turned OFF to the valve, (a switch located just above the valve actuator) the valve is spring closed, and will seal the reactor, and everything upstream.  This equipment will be replaced by the contractor once a replacement is available.
